channel.title: Drupal Core
channel.description: Stay informed about developments in Drupal Core.

item.title: #3564234: Add display-builder agnostic overview page for display modes
item.description:
• Project: Drupal Core
  • Type: Normal task
  • Version: 11.x-dev
  • Status: Fixed
  • Changes: 51 files, +879/−211 · php +813/−211 · css +55 · yml +11
  • Diff: 987768b
  • Discussion: #3564234 · 11 contributors · 53 comments
  • Followers: 23

Summary

The "Manage display" tab in Field UI now opens a display-builder agnostic overview page instead of dropping administrators directly into the default view mode's field formatter form. The overview lists all view modes for a bundle in two tables: "Enabled view modes" and "Disabled view modes". Each enabled mode has a "Manage" link to its configuration form and a "Disable" link; disabled modes have an "Enable" link. The default view mode is always shown as enabled and cannot be disabled. A "Manage view modes" action link leads to the global view modes administration page. The individual display mode forms are unchanged; only the navigation entry point has moved.

Impact

Affects module maintainers and front-end developers.

  • Theme change: New drupal.field_ui_display_overview library and field_ui_display_overview_table.css stylesheet added to the 'Manage display' overview page.
  • New API: New entity.entity_view_display_overview.{entity_type_id} route and EntityViewDisplayOverviewController / EntityViewDisplayStatusController for the display mode overview and toggle actions.

Technical details

Three new routes are registered per entity type in RouteSubscriber::alterRoutes(). The overview route entity.entity_view_display_overview.{entity_type_id} is placed at $path/display and handled by the new EntityViewDisplayOverviewController::overview(). The existing default display route entity.entity_view_display.{entity_type_id}.default was moved from $path/display to $path/display/default. A new CSRF-protected toggle route entity.entity_view_display.{entity_type_id}.toggle_status at $path/display/{view_mode_name}/{operation} is handled by EntityViewDisplayStatusController::toggleStatus(), which enables or disables the named view mode and redirects back to the overview. The default view mode is hardcoded as always enabled; attempting to disable it throws an \InvalidArgumentException.

The "Custom display settings" collapsible section (the checkboxes for enabling view modes) was removed from EntityDisplayFormBase::form() and its corresponding submitForm() logic. That same checkbox UI and submit logic was moved into EntityFormDisplayEditForm, so form modes still use the checkbox pattern on the default form display. View mode enable/disable is now exclusively managed through the overview page.

Local task derivatives in FieldUiLocalTask were updated: display_overview_{entity_type_id} now resolves to the new overview route. A new "Overview" secondary tab field_display_overview_{entity_type_id} was added, and the "Manage display" operation link emitted by entityOperation() in entity listing pages also now targets the overview route. The new drupal.field_ui_display_overview library loads css/field_ui_display_overview_table.css for the overview layout.

Upgrade

Any code or test that navigated to $bundle_path/display and expected the default view mode form must be updated to $bundle_path/display/default.

// Before:
$url = Url::fromRoute("entity.entity_view_display.$entity_type_id.default", $params);

// Still works; no change needed for routes that already use the named route.
// Only bare path strings or links hardcoded to /display are affected.

// Before (hardcoded path):
$this->drupalGet('admin/structure/types/manage/article/display');

// After:
$this->drupalGet('admin/structure/types/manage/article/display/default');

Code that previously submitted the display_modes_custom[{mode}] checkbox on the default view display form to enable a view mode must switch to either clicking the "Enable" link on the overview page or directly creating/saving an entity_view_display config entity with status: TRUE:

$storage = \Drupal::entityTypeManager()->getStorage('entity_view_display');
$display = $storage->create([
  'targetEntityType' => 'node',
  'bundle' => 'article',
  'mode' => 'full',
  'status' => TRUE,
]);
$display->save();

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: December 20, 2025
  • First commit: April 27, 2026 (4 months later)
  • Last commit: May 22, 2026 (25 days and 2 commits later)

Key contributors

  • lauriii (Acquia)
  • grimreaper (Smile)
  • catch (Third and Grove)
  • joachim (Chicken)
  • godotislate
  • benjifisher (Harvard Web Publishing)
  • pallavi singh3013 (OpenSense Labs)
  • rkoller
  • pameeela (Technocrat)
  • smustgrave (Mobomo)
  • dcam

Collaboration

The usability review comment by benjifisher (comment 23) shaped the shipped UI in several concrete ways: it prompted adding visually hidden text to operation links for screen-reader accessibility, changing table headings from "display modes" to "view modes", adding a "Manage view modes" link back to the global view mode administration page (which had previously been removed), and opening a follow-up issue (#3574972) for the horizontal whitespace problem. The pameeela contribution (comment 28) reworked the placement and phrasing of the "Manage view modes" link, and lauriii responded by converting it to a prominent action link displayed above the table, which became the final design.

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.

item.title: #3591179: Update main and 11.x to latest Twig and Symfony versions
item.description:
• Project: Drupal Core
  • Type: Critical task
  • Version: 11.x-dev
  • Status: Fixed
  • Changes: 10 files, +278/−263 · lock +229/−217 · json +46/−46 · php +3
  • Diff: dc3c97a
  • Discussion: #3591179 · 3 contributors · 14 comments
  • Followers: 4

Summary

Following the Drupal 11.3.10 security release, the main and 11.x development branches have been updated to the same patched versions of Twig and Symfony that were shipped in the stable security release. The update raises twig/twig from ^3.21.0 to ^3.26.0 and tightens the minimum constraint for several Symfony 8.0 packages (including http-kernel, mailer, mime, routing, and yaml) to ^8.0.12. Sites on a stable release were already covered by 11.3.10; this change keeps the development branches aligned with those security fixes so contributors are not working against vulnerable dependencies.

Impact

Affects site owners.

  • Security: Twig and Symfony packages updated to security-patched versions matching Drupal 11.3.10; twig/twig minimum raised to 3.26.0 and several symfony/* packages pinned to 8.0.12

Technical details

The commit updates both composer.json version constraints and the resolved composer.lock entries. On the constraint side, twig/twig is moved to ^3.26.0 and five Symfony packages (symfony/http-kernel, symfony/mailer, symfony/mime, symfony/routing, symfony/yaml, and the test-only symfony/dom-crawler) are pinned to ^8.0.12. The remaining Symfony packages receive lock-file-only bumps without constraint changes. Symfony polyfills advance from ~v1.33.0 to ~v1.37.0. The locked symfony/serializer v8.0.10 entry gains a new conflict against symfony/property-access <7.4.2|>=8.0,<8.0.2, and symfony/validator v8.0.10 gains a conflict against symfony/expression-language <7.4, reflecting upstream dependency refinements present in those releases. PHPStan baseline adjustments were also needed to resolve ignore.unmatchedIdentifier errors against the MySQL, PostgreSQL, and SQLite database driver files that appeared after the Symfony upgrade; those changes are part of the same commit.

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: May 20, 2026
  • Committed: May 21, 2026

Key contributors

  • xjm (Zoocha)
  • quietone (PreviousNext)
  • longwave (Full Fat Things)
  • larowlan (PreviousNext)

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.

item.title: #3586654: Avoid scanning the file system for local po files
item.description:
• Project: Drupal Core
  • Type: Normal task
  • Version: 11.x-dev
  • Status: Fixed
  • Changes: 1 file, +4/−11 · php +4/−11
  • Diff: 1d9e127
  • Discussion: #3586654 · 7 contributors · 25 comments
  • Followers: 12

Summary

Sites that check for translation updates via the locale module will see a dramatic performance improvement. On a site with 200 modules and 38 languages, locale_translation_check_projects_local() dropped from around 5 minutes to roughly 7 seconds. The change removes an unnecessary file-system directory scan that was triggered for every project and language combination during a local .po file check.

Impact

No upgrade or configuration changes required.

Technical details

LocaleSource::sourceCheckFile previously located local .po files by calling FileSystemInterface::scanDirectory() with a regex-escaped filename pattern, even though the file's URI was already stored on $source_file->uri. This scan ran for every project-language pair, causing severe I/O and serialization overhead at scale.

The fix replaces the entire directory scan with a direct file_exists($source_file->uri) check. When the URI is present and the file exists, filemtime() and hash_file() are called on the known path. The $source_file->uri assignment that the old scan performed is now skipped because the URI is already populated before sourceCheckFile is called.

The scan was a historical artefact. As traced by comment archaeology against #1804688 (where it was introduced) and #1998056 (where cron-based auto-updates arrived), the original code tracked multiple versions of .po files, which made a scan meaningful. When #1998056 shifted tracking to only the most recent download, the multi-version lookup rationale disappeared but the expensive scan was never removed. Because this functionality had effectively been absent since Drupal 8.8.0 with no reported breakage, removing the scan carries no compatibility risk. Broader optimisations to locale_translation_get_status and the KV store reads are tracked separately in #3037156 and #3590050.

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: April 24, 2026
  • Committed: May 19, 2026 (25 days and 2 commits later)

Key contributors

  • webflo (UEBERBIT GmbH)
  • ishani patel
  • nicxvan (nLightened Development LLC)
  • andypost (Skilld)
  • penyaskito (Acquia)
  • berdir (MD Systems GmbH)

Collaboration

penyaskito spent 1.5 hours tracing the directory-scan code back through #1804688 and #1998056, establishing that the multi-file tracking behaviour that originally justified the scan was silently dropped in 8.8.0 with no visible impact. That historical finding gave the team the confidence to remove the scan outright. berdir later narrowed the issue scope specifically to sourceCheckFile so the fix could land immediately without waiting for the broader locale refactor ongoing in #3037156.

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.

item.title: #3590435: Backport changes to DrupalTestCaseTrait and related classes from 12.x to 11.x
item.description:
• Project: Drupal Core
  • Type: Normal task
  • Version: 11.x-dev
  • Status: Fixed
  • Changes: 3 files, +8/−8 · php +8/−8
  • Diff: d43dcd7
  • Discussion: #3590435 · 4 contributors · 16 comments
  • Followers: 5

Summary

Three test-infrastructure symbols that were originally deprecated as of Drupal 12.0.0 are now correctly deprecated as of Drupal 11.4.0. The symbols are TestRequirementsTrait (the whole trait), TestRequirementsTrait::getDrupalRoot(), and BrowserTestBase::setUpAppRoot(). A matching deprecation exists on DrupalTestCaseTrait::getDrupalRoot(). All are removed in Drupal 13.0.0. If your contributed or custom modules contain test classes that use any of these, you will start seeing deprecation notices on Drupal 11.4 and should migrate to $this->root directly.

Impact

Affects module maintainers.

  • Deprecation: TestRequirementsTrait, TestRequirementsTrait::getDrupalRoot(), DrupalTestCaseTrait::getDrupalRoot(), and BrowserTestBase::setUpAppRoot() are deprecated from drupal:11.4.0 (previously stated as 12.0.0) and removed in drupal:13.0.0; use $this->root directly

Technical details

The original work in #3573954 and #3589047 landed on 12.x using a PHP 8.4 property hook on DrupalTestCaseTrait::$root, which meant the changes could not be backported to Drupal 11 (which still supports PHP 8.3). Issue #3590425 replaced that hook with a compatible initialization, unblocking the backport. This issue then backports the full changeset to 11.x and, on main, corrects the three deprecation @trigger_error strings and docblock annotations from drupal:12.0.0 to drupal:11.4.0 in core/tests/Drupal/Tests/TestRequirementsTrait.php, core/tests/Drupal/Tests/DrupalTestCaseTrait.php, and core/tests/Drupal/Tests/BrowserTestBase.php. The removal target remains drupal:13.0.0 in all cases. The associated change record is #3574112.

Upgrade

Replace any usage of the deprecated symbols in your test classes:

// Before (deprecated as of drupal:11.4.0)
use \Drupal\Tests\TestRequirementsTrait;
$root = self::getDrupalRoot();          // via TestRequirementsTrait or DrupalTestCaseTrait
$this->setUpAppRoot();                  // via BrowserTestBase

// After
$root = $this->root;                    // access the $root property directly

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: May 16, 2026
  • Committed: May 19, 2026 (2 days and 2 commits later)

Key contributors

  • kingdutch (Open Social)
  • mondrake
  • alexpott (Acro Commerce)

Collaboration

mondrake explained in a comment why a single consolidated backport issue was necessary rather than backporting each parent issue individually: the first parent issue (#3573954) used a PHP 8.4 property hook, so backporting it alone to the PHP-8.3-compatible 11.x branch would have left the repository in a broken state between commits. A single squashed backport was the only safe approach.

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.

item.title: #3587707: Try to avoid extra cache sets/gets in ThemeRegistry
item.description:
• Project: Drupal Core
  • Type: Normal task
  • Version: 11.x-dev
  • Status: Fixed
  • Changes: 6 files, +17/−60 · php +17/−60
  • Diff: 3524944
  • Discussion: #3587707 · 4 contributors · 20 comments
  • Followers: 7

Summary

On cold-cache page requests, ThemeRegistry was performing an unnecessary extra cache read and write during its own construction, on top of what the parent CacheCollector class already handles. This has been removed. Sites will see slightly fewer database queries and cache operations on cold-cache requests, with no change in visible behavior.

Impact

No upgrade or configuration changes required.

Technical details

Before this change, ThemeRegistry::__construct() hard-set $this->cacheLoaded = TRUE and manually issued a $this->cache->get() to populate $this->storage, bypassing CacheCollector's lazy-loading mechanism entirely. It also called $this->updateCache() at the end of construction, causing an immediate cache write (with a lock acquire and release against the semaphore table) every time the theme registry was instantiated without a warm cache.

The fix replaces the manual constructor logic with a call to $this->lazyLoadCache(), the standard CacheCollector method. The full custom updateCache() override (about 25 lines covering lock acquisition, cache merging, and write) was also removed; ThemeRegistry::updateCache() now just calls parent::updateCache($lock). The only logic retained in the constructor is the initialization of keysToPersist for each registry key when the storage is empty after the lazy load.

As a side effect, the semaphore lock key for the theme registry changes from theme_registry:runtime:<theme>:Drupal\Core\Utility\ThemeRegistry to theme_registry:runtime:<theme>:Drupal\Core\Cache\CacheCollector, matching the pattern used by all other CacheCollector subclasses. Performance test fixtures in NodePageCacheTest, FrontPageTest, and the Umami performance tests were updated to reflect the reduced query and cache operation counts (typically 2 fewer queries, 1 fewer cache get, and 1 fewer cache set per cold-cache request).

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: April 29, 2026
  • First commit: May 18, 2026 (18 days later)
  • Last commit: May 19, 2026 (1 day and 2 commits later)

Key contributors

  • catch (Third and Grove)

Collaboration

catch discovered the redundancy by running dump($this->getCacheOperations()) against the node cold-cache performance test and observing the theme registry cache entry being read at least three times and written twice per request. He noted that the pre-emptive cache write was added in 2013 and that CacheCollector had since accumulated sufficient race-condition protection to make it unnecessary. He also identified that the full updateCache() override was superfluous and simplified it to a parent call in the same pass.

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.

item.title: #3590425: Remove usage of property hook for DrupalTestCaseTrait::$root
item.description:
• Project: Drupal Core
  • Type: Normal task
  • Version: main
  • Status: Fixed
  • Changes: 1 file, +16/−8 · php +16/−8
  • Discussion: #3590425 · 3 contributors · 10 comments
  • Followers: 4

Summary

In Drupal's PHPUnit test infrastructure, the $root property on DrupalTestCaseTrait was previously initialized via a PHP 8.4 property hook. That hook has been replaced with a plain property initialized by a new #[Before(100)] method called setUpRoot(). The change makes the code backportable to 11.x (where property hooks are not available) and ensures $root is reliably populated before any other #[Before] hooks or setUp() calls execute. This is a main-only change; backporting is tracked in #3590435.

Impact

No upgrade or configuration changes required.

Technical details

The previous implementation used a PHP 8.4 hooked property: protected string $root { get { ... } }, which initialized lazily on first read. This required a phpcs:ignoreFile directive because PHP_CodeSniffer did not yet support property hook syntax. The replacement uses a plain protected string $root; declaration combined with a final #[Before(100)] method setUpRoot() that performs eager initialization before any other test setup runs. The high priority value 100 ensures setUpRoot() fires before other #[Before] hooks and before setUp().

The method is declared final to block subclasses from overriding it, which would bypass the single intended initialization. If $root is already set when setUpRoot() is invoked, it throws a \LogicException with the message "setUpRoot should be called exactly once by PHPUnit's Before test hook and root overrides should happen after." This guards against pre-assignment of $root in a subclass constructor or property initializer. The method is marked @internal.

This change is part of a broader consolidation of test base-class logic into DrupalTestCaseTrait (#3573954) and the deprecation of TestRequirementsTrait::getDrupalRoot() (#3589047). The plan documented in the issue body is to re-introduce a property hook once backporting to 11.x is no longer needed, at which point setUpRoot() can be removed.

Upgrade

If a custom or contrib test class that uses DrupalTestCaseTrait (or a base class that uses it) assigns $this->root or declares protected string $root = '...' before PHPUnit runs the #[Before] hooks, that test will now throw a \LogicException. Remove any such pre-assignment; $root is initialized automatically by setUpRoot() before setUp() runs.

// Before (would now cause a LogicException at runtime):
class MyTest extends BrowserTestBase {
  protected string $root = '/custom/path'; // pre-assignment
}

// After: do not pre-set $root; override it in setUp() after parent::setUp() if needed.

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: May 16, 2026

Key contributors

  • kingdutch (Open Social)
  • mondrake

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.

item.title: #1452100: Private file download returns access denied, when file attached to revision other than current
item.description:
• Project: Drupal Core
  • Type: Major bug report
  • Version: 11.x-dev
  • Status: Fixed
  • Changes: 11 files, +521/−25 · php +520/−25 · yml +1
  • Diff: c31deda
  • Discussion: #1452100 · 61 contributors · 217 comments
  • Followers: 110

Summary

Private files attached to non-default entity revisions (draft revisions created by Content Moderation, or older revisions accessible via revision history) previously returned "Access Denied" to all users, including administrators. This happened because the file download hook and access handler only checked the default revision for a file reference. If a file had been replaced in the current revision, or existed only in a pending draft, no reference was found and access was denied. The fix makes private file access work correctly: users with the view revision permission on the referencing entity can now download private files from non-default revisions, while users without that permission continue to be denied access to files that are not in the current revision.

Impact

Affects module maintainers.

  • Deprecation: file_get_file_references(), file_field_find_file_reference_column(), and FileAccessControlHandler::getFileReferences() deprecated in drupal:11.4.0, removed in drupal:13.0.0; use FileReferenceResolver::getReferences() instead.
  • New API: New FileReferenceResolver service and FileReferenceUsage value object for resolving file references across both default and non-default entity revisions.

Technical details

The old file_get_file_references() function loaded only the current/default revision of each referencing entity. When a file was superseded in the current revision or existed only in a pending draft, no reference was found and access was denied. The file_file_download() hook compounded this by also using FIELD_LOAD_CURRENT for its early-exit reference check.

The fix introduces FileReferenceResolver (registered as a service in file.services.yml) and a FileReferenceUsage readonly value object. getReferences(FileInterface $file) is a generator that implements a two-pass strategy: first it loads the default revision of each entity recorded in file_usage and checks all file-reference fields (across all translations); if the file is not found in the default revision and the entity type is revisionable, it queries allRevisions() sorted descending to find the most recent non-default revision that references the file. Results are capped at 50 non-default revision lookups per entity type (REVISION_LOOKUP_LIMIT). Results are cached in the memory cache under the file_references cache tag. Each yielded FileReferenceUsage carries the entity type ID, field name, and either the entity ID (default-revision match) or the revision ID (non-default-revision match).

FileAccessControlHandler::checkAccess() now calls $resolver->getReferences() and $resolver->loadEntityFromUsage() instead of the old getFileReferences() method. When the loaded entity implements RevisionableInterface and !isDefaultRevision(), the handler checks view revision access rather than view access before additionally checking field-level access. FileDownloadHook::fileDownload() also uses the resolver for its early-exit check (consuming only the first yielded result).

file_get_file_references(), file_field_find_file_reference_column(), and FileAccessControlHandler::getFileReferences() are all deprecated in drupal:11.4.0 and removed in drupal:13.0.0. Cache invalidation via drupal_static_reset('file_get_file_references') or drupal_static_reset('file_get_file_references:field_columns') is also deprecated.

Upgrade

// Before (deprecated in 11.4.0, removed in 13.0.0):
use Drupal\Core\Entity\EntityStorageInterface;
$references = file_get_file_references($file, NULL, EntityStorageInterface::FIELD_LOAD_REVISION, NULL);

// After:
$resolver = \Drupal::service(\Drupal\file\FileReferenceResolver::class);
foreach ($resolver->getReferences($file) as $usage) {
  $entity = $resolver->loadEntityFromUsage($usage);
  // $usage->entityTypeId, $usage->fieldName, $usage->id, $usage->revisionId
}

// Before (deprecated):
drupal_static_reset('file_get_file_references');

// After:
\Drupal::service('cache.memory')->invalidateTags(['file_references']);

file_field_find_file_reference_column() is deprecated with no replacement.

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: February 23, 2012
  • Committed: May 16, 2026 (14 years and 2 commits later)

Key contributors

  • jlongbottom
  • berdir (MD Systems GmbH)
  • therainmakor
  • gapple
  • benjy (PreviousNext)
  • cilefen
  • eiriksm (Ny Media AS)
  • cristiroma
  • leontin
  • amateescu
  • drenton
  • shubhangi1995
  • acbramley (PreviousNext)
  • catch (Third and Grove)
  • alexpott (Acro Commerce)
  • fenstrat (PreviousNext)
  • larowlan (PreviousNext)
  • mxr576 (Pronovix)
  • rakshith.thotada
  • kim.pepper (PreviousNext)
  • richard.thomas
  • effulgentsia (Acquia)
  • lauriii (Acquia)
  • xjm (xjm)
  • nicxvan (nLightened Development LLC)

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.

item.title: #3590237: Add PHP 8.6 polyfill
item.description:
• Project: Drupal Core
  • Type: Normal task
  • Version: 11.x-dev
  • Status: Fixed
  • Changes: 3 files, +84/−1 · lock +82/−1 · json +2
  • Diff: c78fd35
  • Discussion: #3590237 · 5 contributors · 18 comments
  • Followers: 7

Summary

Drupal core now ships symfony/polyfill-php86 (v1.37), making two PHP 8.6 additions available on sites running older PHP versions: the clamp() function and the SortDirection enum. Contrib module authors and custom code developers can use these features today without gating on the PHP version of the host environment.

Impact

Affects module maintainers.

  • New API: PHP 8.6 clamp() function and SortDirection enum are now available on all supported PHP versions via symfony/polyfill-php86 v1.37

Technical details

The diff adds "symfony/polyfill-php86": "^1.37" to core/composer.json and pins it to ~v1.37.0 in the root composer/composer.json. The package's bootstrap.php is autoloaded via Composer's files mechanism, so the polyfill is always active regardless of the PHP version. On PHP 8.6+ the package is a no-op; on earlier versions it provides the clamp() global function and the SortDirection enum stub under Symfony\Polyfill\Php86. No existing core code was changed to use these symbols in this commit; the dependency was added to unblock contrib and to simplify backporting MRs that target main (or 11.x) to older branches.

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: May 14, 2026
  • Committed: May 16, 2026 (1 day and 2 commits later)

Key contributors

  • andypost (Skilld)

Collaboration

A brief design question arose: dww asked whether at least one usage should be added in core so tests confirm the new functions work and the dependency is not unused. mstrelan countered that previous polyfills (such as the PHP 8.3 one) landed without immediate usage and proved useful precisely for backporting MRs and for contrib adoption. dww accepted that reasoning and the issue proceeded to commit without any in-core usage.

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.

item.title: #3516173: Block status code visibility condition should use a status code cache context
item.description:
• Project: Drupal Core
  • Type: Normal task
  • Version: 11.x-dev
  • Status: Fixed
  • Changes: 4 files, +104/−1 · php +99/−1 · yml +5
  • Diff: ce37b06
  • Discussion: #3516173 · 5 contributors · 31 comments
  • Followers: 9

Summary

The block "response status code" visibility condition (introduced in #2245767 to show or hide blocks based on 200/403/404 response pages) was using the url.path cache context. Because 404 pages typically have unique paths, every distinct 404 URL visited generated its own dynamic page cache entry, rapidly bloating the cache. This change replaces that context with a new, coarser exception_status_code cache context so that all requests sharing the same HTTP error code (e.g. all 404s) can reuse a single cache entry, dramatically reducing cache table growth on sites with many 404s.

Impact

Affects module maintainers.

  • New API: New exception_status_code cache context (ExceptionStatusCodeCacheContext) for varying cache entries by HTTP exception status code.

Technical details

The HttpResponseCodeCondition plugin's getCacheContexts() method previously appended url.path to its declared cache contexts. The fix replaces this with exception_status_code.

The new ExceptionStatusCodeCacheContext class (registered as cache_context.exception_status_code) extends RequestStackCacheContextBase and reads the exception attribute from the current request. HttpExceptionSubscriberBase sets this attribute before block rendering, so it is reliably available. If the attribute holds an instance of HttpExceptionInterface, the context returns the status code as a string (e.g. '404', '403'). If there is no exception or it is not an HttpExceptionInterface, it returns '0' to represent a normal (typically 200) response. Returning '0' rather than '200' avoids falsely claiming knowledge of what response subscribers may set.

Upgrade

No upgrade steps are required for existing code. If you maintain a custom cache context or condition plugin that currently uses url.path purely to vary by error status, consider switching to exception_status_code instead.

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: March 29, 2025
  • Committed: May 15, 2026 (1 year and 2 commits later)

Key contributors

  • catch (Third and Grove)
  • alexpott (Acro Commerce)
  • godotislate

Collaboration

alexpott questioned whether the cache context could work reliably given that the response code is determined after the request, not from it. catch explained that HttpExceptionSubscriberBase sets the exception as a request attribute before block rendering, making it available at context evaluation time. Later, alexpott asked what should happen when the exception attribute exists but is not an HttpExceptionInterface. catch added an instanceof check and confirmed that the existing fallback to '0' covers that case.

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.

item.title: #3581303: Convert locale batch callbacks
item.description:
• Project: Drupal Core
  • Type: Normal task
  • Version: 11.x-dev
  • Status: Fixed
  • Changes: 24 files, +1142/−608 · php +1137/−608 · yml +5
  • Diff: 16e91ba
  • Discussion: #3581303 · 3 contributors · 36 comments
  • Followers: 5

Summary

All batch-related procedural functions in the locale module's .inc files are now deprecated in Drupal 11.4.0 and will be removed in 13.0.0. The logic has been moved into three injectable services: the existing LocaleFetch and LocaleProjectChecker services (which gained new methods) and two new services, LocaleConfigBatch and LocaleImportBatch. Custom modules, install profiles, or any code that calls the old procedural callbacks directly will trigger deprecation warnings and must be updated before upgrading to Drupal 13.

Impact

Affects module maintainers.

  • Deprecation: Nineteen procedural batch callback functions across locale.batch.inc, locale.bulk.inc, and locale.compare.inc deprecated in 11.4.0, removed in 13.0.0; replacements are methods on LocaleFetch, LocaleProjectChecker, LocaleConfigBatch, and LocaleImportBatch services.
  • New API: Two new services added: Drupal\locale\LocaleConfigBatch and Drupal\locale\LocaleImportBatch.

Technical details

The change spans three .inc files (locale.batch.inc, locale.bulk.inc, locale.compare.inc) and affects nineteen procedural functions. Each function now contains only a @trigger_error() deprecation notice and a delegation call to the equivalent service method. Two new service classes are introduced: LocaleConfigBatch (handles config translation batch operations previously in locale_config_batch_* functions) and LocaleImportBatch (handles translation file import batch operations previously in locale_translate_batch_* functions). Both are registered in locale.services.yml without autowiring on 11.x. The existing LocaleFetch service gained batchVersionCheck(), batchStatusCheck(), batchDownload(), batchImport(), batchFinished(), and getStatusOperations() methods. LocaleProjectChecker gained triggerBatch() and batchFinished().

Two methods on LocaleFetch were also renamed as part of this commit: batchUpdateBuild() → buildUpdateBatch() and batchFetchBuild() → buildFetchBatch(). The deprecated procedural wrappers locale_translation_batch_update_build() and locale_translation_batch_fetch_build() (from a prior issue, #3577671) had their deprecation notice text updated to reflect the new names. Internal callers in locale.module, install.core.inc, ImportForm, and TranslationStatusForm are updated to use the service methods directly. ImportForm and TranslationStatusForm were also modernized to use constructor property promotion and inject the new services. locale_config_batch_build() is deprecated but retains a partially delegated implementation that uses LocaleConfigBatch::class . ':batchUpdateConfigTranslations' as batch operation strings, enabled by the BatchBuilder accepting plain strings (see #3575096).

Upgrade

Replace calls to the procedural functions with calls to the equivalent service methods. The change record URL is https://www.drupal.org/node/3589759.

locale.batch.inc functions:

// Before
locale_translation_batch_version_check($project, $langcode, $context);
locale_translation_batch_status_check($project, $langcode, $options, $context);
locale_translation_batch_fetch_download($project, $langcode, $context);
locale_translation_batch_fetch_import($project, $langcode, $options, $context);
locale_translation_batch_fetch_finished($success, $results);
locale_translation_batch_status_finished($success, $results);
_locale_translation_batch_status_operations($projects, $langcodes, $options);

// After
\Drupal::service(\Drupal\locale\LocaleFetch::class)->batchVersionCheck($project, $langcode, $context);
\Drupal::service(\Drupal\locale\LocaleFetch::class)->batchStatusCheck($project, $langcode, $options, $context);
\Drupal::service(\Drupal\locale\LocaleFetch::class)->batchDownload($project, $langcode, $context);
\Drupal::service(\Drupal\locale\LocaleFetch::class)->batchImport($project, $langcode, $options, $context);
\Drupal::service(\Drupal\locale\LocaleFetch::class)->batchFinished($success, $results);
\Drupal::service(\Drupal\locale\LocaleProjectChecker::class)->batchFinished($success, $results);
\Drupal::service(\Drupal\locale\LocaleFetch::class)->getStatusOperations($projects, $langcodes, $options);

locale.bulk.inc functions:

// Before
locale_translate_batch_build($files, $options);
locale_translate_batch_import($file, $options, $context);
locale_translate_batch_import_save($context);
locale_translate_batch_refresh($context);
locale_translate_batch_finished($success, $results);
locale_config_batch_update_components($options, $langcodes, $components, $update_default_config_langcodes);
locale_config_batch_update_default_config_langcodes($context);
locale_config_batch_update_config_translations($names, $langcodes, $context);
locale_config_batch_finished($success, $results);

// After
\Drupal::service(\Drupal\locale\LocaleImportBatch::class)->buildBatch($files, $options);
\Drupal::service(\Drupal\locale\LocaleImportBatch::class)->batchImport($file, $options, $context);
\Drupal::service(\Drupal\locale\LocaleImportBatch::class)->batchSave($context);
\Drupal::service(\Drupal\locale\LocaleImportBatch::class)->batchRefresh($context);
\Drupal::service(\Drupal\locale\LocaleImportBatch::class)->batchFinished($success, $results);
\Drupal::service(\Drupal\locale\LocaleConfigBatch::class)->buildBatch($options, $langcodes, $components, $update_default_config_langcodes);
\Drupal::service(\Drupal\locale\LocaleConfigBatch::class)->batchUpdateDefaultConfigLangcodes($context);
\Drupal::service(\Drupal\locale\LocaleConfigBatch::class)->batchUpdateConfigTranslations($names, $langcodes, $context);
\Drupal::service(\Drupal\locale\LocaleConfigBatch::class)->batchFinished($success, $results);

locale.compare.inc functions:

// Before
locale_translation_check_projects_batch($projects, $langcodes);
locale_translation_batch_status_build($projects, $langcodes);

// After
\Drupal::service(\Drupal\locale\LocaleProjectChecker::class)->triggerBatch($projects, $langcodes);
// locale_translation_batch_status_build() has no direct equivalent;
// use LocaleProjectChecker::triggerBatch() which calls batch_set() internally.

LocaleFetch method renames (already deprecated via #3577671 but names corrected here):

// Before
\Drupal::service(LocaleFetch::class)->batchUpdateBuild($projects, $langcodes, $options);
\Drupal::service(LocaleFetch::class)->batchFetchBuild($projects, $langcodes, $options);

// After
\Drupal::service(LocaleFetch::class)->buildUpdateBatch($projects, $langcodes, $options);
\Drupal::service(LocaleFetch::class)->buildFetchBatch($projects, $langcodes, $options);

Batch callback strings used in BatchBuilder::addOperation() must also be updated:

// Before (procedural callback strings)
$batch_builder->addOperation('locale_translate_batch_import', [$file, $options]);
$batch_builder->setFinishCallback('locale_translate_batch_finished');

// After (service method strings)
$batch_builder->addOperation(\Drupal\locale\LocaleImportBatch::class . ':batchImport', [$file, $options]);
$batch_builder->setFinishCallback(\Drupal\locale\LocaleImportBatch::class . ':batchFinished');

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: March 25, 2026
  • Committed: May 15, 2026 (1 month and 2 commits later)

Key contributors

  • nicxvan (nLightened Development LLC)
  • berdir (MD Systems GmbH)
  • amateescu
  • longwave (Full Fat Things)

Collaboration

berdir provided the key design direction in comment #16583848: rather than splitting callbacks by type (operations, finishers), group them by logical batch, and integrate fetch-related callbacks into existing services while creating a new service for config-related ones. This redirected the initial approach and led to the four-service grouping that shipped. In comment #16587531, berdir noted that with this MR all five .inc files in locale are now 100% deprecated, with only functions in locale.module remaining.

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.

item.title: #3579253: run-tests.sh does not properly process PHPUnit output when no test are executed for a class
item.description:
• Project: Drupal Core
  • Type: Normal bug report
  • Version: 11.x-dev
  • Status: Fixed
  • Changes: 2 files, +53/−47 · php +53/−47
  • Diff: f9b616d
  • Discussion: #3579253 · 4 contributors · 16 comments
  • Followers: 5

Summary

When PHPUnit skips an entire test class because a PHP extension it requires is not installed (e.g. via #[RequiresPhpExtension('apcu')]), it exits with a success code and emits "No tests executed!" rather than reporting individual test results. Drupal's run-tests.sh wrapper was treating this as a passing test run. The fix makes run-tests.sh treat zero collected test results as an error condition, the same way it already treats deprecations or warnings, so developers running the test suite on machines with missing extensions see a clear failure instead of a silent false positive.

Impact

No upgrade or configuration changes required.

Technical details

The affected class is PhpUnitTestRunner. In processPhpUnitOnSingleTestClassOutcome, the condition that triggers error output was extended from $status > TestStatus::PASS to also fire when $phpunit_results === [], covering the case where PHPUnit produced no result records because it never ran any tests.

Because summarizeResults previously built its result map by iterating over $phpunit_results and reading $result['test_class'] from each entry, an empty results array produced an empty summary map with no entry for the class under test. The method now accepts the class name as a required first argument (string $test_class) and pre-initializes the summary entry for that class before iterating, ensuring an entry always exists even when results are empty. The test data provider providerTestSummarizeResults was also refactored from a plain array return to a PHP generator (\Generator) to match the new three-argument test method signature.

Upgrade

If custom code calls PhpUnitTestRunner::summarizeResults() directly, note that the method is marked @internal but its signature changed: a string $test_class argument is now required as the first parameter.

// Before
$summaries = $runner->summarizeResults($phpunit_results);

// After
$summaries = $runner->summarizeResults($test_class, $phpunit_results);

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: March 14, 2026
  • Committed: May 14, 2026 (2 months and 2 commits later)

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.

item.title: #455724: Deprecate check_markup()
item.description:
• Project: Drupal Core
  • Type: Normal task
  • Version: 11.x-dev
  • Status: Fixed
  • Changes: 14 files, +159/−46 · php +159/−46
  • Diff: a69f1e9
  • Discussion: #455724 · 19 contributors · 132 comments
  • Followers: 25

Summary

The check_markup() function in filter.module is deprecated as of Drupal 11.4.0 and will be removed in Drupal 13.0.0. The function has no direct procedural replacement. The recommended approach is to return a processed_text render array directly rather than flattening it to a markup string, because flattening discards cacheability metadata (cache tags, contexts, max-age) that filters can attach. Module code that currently calls check_markup() and renders the result must be updated before upgrading to Drupal 13.

Impact

Affects module maintainers.

  • Deprecation: check_markup() in filter.module is deprecated in drupal:11.4.0 and removed in drupal:13.0.0; use a processed_text render array instead.

Technical details

The function check_markup() was a thin convenience wrapper: it built a ['#type' => 'processed_text', '#text' => ..., '#format' => ..., ...] render array and immediately flattened it to a MarkupInterface string via the renderer. That flattening step is the problem, because it silently drops any cacheability metadata that filter plugins attach during processing.

The commit adds a @trigger_error() deprecation notice at the top of check_markup() and documents the deprecation in the docblock. There is explicitly no new service or function provided as a replacement; callers are expected to pass the processed_text render array further up the stack and let the rendering system handle it.

The one production caller updated in this commit is the Views MarkupField handler (core/modules/views/src/Plugin/views/field/MarkupField.php), which now returns a processed_text render array directly. Test code that needed to assert on string output was updated to use a new test-only trait Drupal\Tests\filter\Traits\ProcessedTextTestTrait, whose processText() helper replicates what check_markup() did but is explicitly documented as unsuitable for production use. The issue was originally filed in 2009 as a renaming request, and was rescoped to a full deprecation as part of the broader meta effort to eliminate core .module procedural files (#3566536).

Upgrade

Replace calls to check_markup() with a processed_text render array and let Drupal's render pipeline handle it:

// Before (deprecated):
$output = check_markup($text, $format_id, $langcode, $filter_types_to_skip);

// After:
$output = [
  '#type' => 'processed_text',
  '#text' => $text,
  '#format' => $format_id,
  '#langcode' => $langcode,
  '#filter_types_to_skip' => $filter_types_to_skip,
];

Return the render array from your method or template variable rather than casting it to a string. If you must obtain a string for an isolated test assertion, use the Drupal\Tests\filter\Traits\ProcessedTextTestTrait helper in your test class — but do not use that pattern in production code.

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: May 6, 2009
  • Committed: May 13, 2026 (17 years and 2 commits later)

Key contributors

  • greggles
  • cburschka
  • heine
  • catch (Third and Grove)
  • sun
  • agentrickard
  • claudiu.cristea (Webikon)
  • nicxvan (nLightened Development LLC)
  • berdir (MD Systems GmbH)
  • alexpott (Acro Commerce)

Collaboration

claudiu.cristea reopened work on the issue in 2025, noting that check_markup() had roughly 450 known usages in contributed projects. In a series of comments they weighed whether the logic should move into the renderer service, a new dedicated service, or simply be dropped with no replacement. They concluded that the two-line body of check_markup() did not warrant a new service and that the correct guidance was to avoid flattening the render array at all, which led to the "no direct replacement" framing that appears in the final deprecation message.

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.

item.title: #3588331: PsrResponseSubscriber doesn't support \Psr\Http\Message\StreamInterface
item.description:
• Project: Drupal Core
  • Type: Major bug report
  • Version: 11.3.x-dev
  • Status: Fixed
  • Changes: 2 files, +56/−2 · php +56/−2
  • Diff: 88b77e6
  • Discussion: #3588331 · 3 contributors · 9 comments
  • Followers: 4

Summary

Drupal controllers that return PSR-7 responses backed by non-seekable stream bodies (such as those produced by the MCP SDK or other third-party streaming SDKs) would previously respond incorrectly because PsrResponseSubscriber never told the Symfony bridge to treat the response as streamed. The subscriber now inspects the PSR-7 body's seekability and passes the appropriate flag, so non-seekable streams are converted to a proper Symfony StreamedResponse.

Impact

No upgrade or configuration changes required.

Technical details

PsrResponseSubscriber::onKernelView() previously called HttpFoundationFactoryInterface::createResponse($controller_result) with no second argument, meaning $streamed always defaulted to false. The Symfony bridge's createResponse uses that flag to decide whether to produce a StreamedResponse (which reads the body lazily) or a regular Response (which buffers the body). Non-seekable PSR-7 streams cannot be rewound after reading, so buffering them silently produces an empty or corrupt response body.

The fix replaces the hard-coded call with $streamed = !$controller_result->getBody()->isSeekable() and passes that value as the second argument. Seekable bodies continue to produce a regular Response; non-seekable bodies now produce a StreamedResponse. Two new unit tests cover the two branches using StreamInterface mocks with controlled isSeekable() return values and mock expectations on createResponse.

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: May 4, 2026
  • Committed: May 13, 2026 (8 days and 3 commits later)

Key contributors

  • mglaman (Acquia)

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.

item.title: #3586470: Support preloading of fonts declared from components
item.description:
• Project: Drupal Core
  • Type: Normal feature request
  • Version: main
  • Status: Fixed
  • Changes: 4 files, +19/−4 · php +16/−4 · yml +3 · woff2 +0
  • Diff: c0f1c27
  • Discussion: #3586470 · 3 contributors · 13 comments
  • Followers: 6

Summary

Single Directory Components (SDC) now support font preloading via the libraryOverrides key in a component's definition. Before this fix, declaring a fonts entry in a component's libraryOverrides would not work correctly because the path translation step ignored font entries. Component authors can now add a fonts block with relative paths and preload: true, and Drupal will resolve those paths and emit the appropriate preload headers just as it does for fonts declared in regular .libraries.yml files.

Impact

Affects module maintainers.

  • New API: SDC libraryOverrides now accepts a fonts key with relative paths and preload: true, enabling font preloading from Single Directory Components.

Technical details

ComponentPluginManager::translateLibraryPaths() was responsible for rewriting component-relative file paths to paths that the core library system can resolve. It handled css and js entries, but the fonts key introduced by #3366561 was never included, so font paths stayed component-relative and never reached the preload pipeline.

The fix adds fonts to the same path-translation loop. The fonts key is unset from the passthrough overrides array, each font filename is checked for UrlHelper::isExternal(), relative filenames are expanded to an absolute path and then converted to a library-root-relative path via makePathRelativeToLibraryRoot(), and the translated entry is written back into $altered_overrides['fonts']. External font URLs are passed through unchanged. A test component fixture (lib-overrides) was extended with a test-component-font.woff2 entry (preload: true) and the functional test testLibraryOverrides() now asserts the font filename appears in the rendered page output.

Upgrade

To preload a font from an SDC component, add a fonts block to the component's libraryOverrides in its component definition file:

libraryOverrides:
  fonts:
    my-font.woff2:
      preload: true

The path is relative to the component directory. External URLs are also supported and passed through unchanged.

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: April 23, 2026
  • Committed: May 13, 2026 (19 days and 2 commits later)

Key contributors

  • grimreaper
  • catch (Third and Grove)

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.

item.title: #3301239: Improve the error message thrown in PoStreamReader::readLine() when translations cannot be saved
item.description:
• Project: Drupal Core
  • Type: Normal bug report
  • Version: 11.3.x-dev
  • Status: Fixed
  • Changes: 2 files, +36/−5 · php +36/−5
  • Diff: 79cbaf5
  • Discussion: #3301239 · 42 contributors · 94 comments
  • Followers: 51

Summary

On multilingual Drupal sites, enabling a module while the interface translations directory (translations://) is misconfigured or unwriteable previously caused a fatal PHP error: TypeError: fgets(): Argument #1 ($stream) must be of type resource, bool given. This would crash Drush or a deployment pipeline mid-run. The fix makes PoStreamReader throw a catchable RuntimeException instead, so the locale subsystem can log a clear message and continue rather than halting with an unhandled fatal error.

Impact

Affects module maintainers.

  • API change: PoStreamReader::open() now throws \RuntimeException when fopen fails to open the stream, instead of silently setting the internal file descriptor to false; callers that invoke open() directly should wrap it in a try/catch.

Technical details

In PoStreamReader::open(), the original code called fopen() but never checked its return value. When fopen fails (for example because translations:// is not properly configured), it returns false. A subsequent call to readLine() then passed that false value directly to fgets(), which in PHP 8 throws a TypeError.

The fix restructures open(): it calls @fopen($this->uri, 'rb') (suppressing the PHP-level warning that would otherwise be emitted) and immediately checks the result. If the handle is falsy, it throws new \RuntimeException('Cannot open stream for uri ' . $this->uri). This gives calling code a catchable, typed exception rather than an unhandled fatal. A new PoStreamReaderTest unit test was added in core/tests/Drupal/Tests/Component/Gettext/PoStreamReaderTest.php to verify this path.

The related issue #3546047 was opened separately to improve the upstream logging in locale_translation_download_source() when the download itself fails, covering the complementary failure mode.

Upgrade

If you call PoStreamReader::open() directly, wrap it to handle the new failure mode:

// Before: open() would silently succeed even if fopen() failed.
$reader->setURI($uri);
$reader->open();

// After: open() throws \RuntimeException when the stream cannot be opened.
$reader->setURI($uri);
try {
  $reader->open();
}
catch (\RuntimeException $e) {
  // Handle misconfigured translations directory or unreadable file.
  \Drupal::logger('mymodule')->error($e->getMessage());
}

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: August 2, 2022
  • Committed: May 12, 2026 (3 years and 3 commits later)

Key contributors

  • immaculatexavier (Material)
  • iperiba92
  • smustgrave (Mobomo)
  • anybody (DROWL.de)
  • rcodina (Seidor)
  • matiasmiranda (Phase2)
  • pfrenssen (Liip)
  • vladimiraus (Tomato Elephant Studio)
  • hchonov (1xINTERNET)
  • i-grou
  • guiu.rocafort.ferrer (Randstad Digital)
  • teknocat
  • oily
  • longwave (Full Fat Things)
  • grevil (DROWL.de)
  • alexpott (Acro Commerce)

Collaboration

The issue sat for nearly three years, accumulating many "patch works for me" comments without ever landing, largely because early patches simply swallowed the error rather than surfacing a useful message. The discussion shifted when pfrenssen (comment #30) and joachim (comment #29) argued that the right fix was an improved error, not silent suppression. guiu.rocafort.ferrer revived the issue on 11.x, reproduced the failure, and proposed throwing a RuntimeException from open() rather than only patching readLine(). pfrenssen then fixed a failing test that arose because PHP itself was emitting a warning that interfered with PHPUnit. vladimiraus contributed a refactor of the approach, and rcodina fixed the remaining pipeline failures before the issue was finally committed.

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.

item.title: #3587298: RouteProcessorCsrf::processOutbound() should bubble 'session' cache context for non-HTML requests
item.description:
• Project: Drupal Core
  • Type: Major bug report
  • Version: 11.x-dev
  • Status: Fixed
  • Changes: 8 files, +88/−4 · php +88/−4
  • Diff: c280911
  • Discussion: #3587298 · 5 contributors · 18 comments
  • Followers: 6

Summary

A bug in RouteProcessorCsrf::processOutbound() caused REST and JSON:API responses that embed CSRF-protected URLs (for example, a logout link) to be cached without varying by session. Two users sharing the same role or other common cache context could receive each other's CSRF tokens from the cache. The fix is targeted at Drupal 11.4.0 (committed to main and 11.x). Sites on 11.3.x are not yet patched in an official release.

Impact

Affects site owners.

  • Security: CSRF tokens generated for non-HTML requests (REST, JSON:API) were not varied by session, allowing a cached response to serve one user's token to another user sharing the same role or other common cache context.

Technical details

The non-HTML branch of processOutbound() generates a real CSRF token via $this->csrfToken->get($path) and injects it as a ?token= query parameter. The behavior of returning a real token (rather than a render placeholder) was introduced by #3485174. That change did not also call $bubbleable_metadata->addCacheContexts(['session']), so callers assembling a cacheable REST or JSON:API response never received session among the required vary contexts. The HTML branch was already correct: its lazy-builder render callback declared '#cache' => ['contexts' => ['session']] (lines 79-81 of RouteProcessorCsrf).

The production fix is a single line added immediately after the token is set: $bubbleable_metadata?->addCacheContexts(['session']). The nullsafe operator means the call is a no-op when $bubbleable_metadata is null, preserving existing behavior for callers that do not pass metadata.

Test changes mirror the real-world impact. Several REST and JSON:API functional test classes now override getExpectedCacheContexts() to include session. LinksetControllerTest::testUserAccountMenu() adds session to expected cacheability because the account menu exposes the CSRF-protected /user/logout URL for authenticated users. A new normalizeHeadersForGetHeadComparison() hook was introduced in the REST test base and overridden in BasicAuthResourceTestTrait to neutralize ?token= values in Link headers: with stateless basic_auth, the CSRF seed is regenerated on every request, so HEAD and GET legitimately produce different token strings even after the fix. The unit test in RouteProcessorCsrfTest gained a CacheContextsManager stub (required for BubbleableMetadata::addCacheContexts() to pass its internal validation) and a direct assertion that ['session'] is present in the returned metadata.

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: April 28, 2026
  • First commit: May 7, 2026 (9 days later)
  • Last commit: May 12, 2026 (4 days and 2 commits later)

Key contributors

  • petar_basic (drunomics)

Collaboration

petar_basic discovered the bug on a vanilla 11.3.7 install, reproduced it by extending the existing unit test, validated the fix end-to-end with two real users, and opened the initial MR. After smustgrave flagged that the pipeline was failing, petar_basic investigated and found the failures were themselves evidence of the bug: Basic Auth functional tests only passed before because the dynamic page cache was serving one session's cached response, including its CSRF tokens, to a different session's request. petar_basic updated the REST fixtures, added the normalizeHeadersForGetHeadComparison() hook, and added the unit-test stub to produce a green pipeline. fago reviewed and confirmed correctness, noting that the API response correctly surfaced the session cache context header after the change.

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.

item.title: #2844620: Automatically split cache debug headers into multiple lines when they exceed 8k
item.description:
• Project: Drupal Core
  • Type: Normal bug report
  • Version: 11.x-dev
  • Status: Fixed
  • Changes: 4 files, +138/−0 · php +118 · yml +20
  • Diff: 024fc72
  • Discussion: #2844620 · 65 contributors · 187 comments
  • Followers: 156

Summary

When http.response.debug_cacheability_headers is set to true in development.services.yml, Drupal emits X-Drupal-Cache-Tags and X-Drupal-Cache-Contexts response headers listing every cache tag and context that bubbled up to the page. On complex pages with many paragraphs, taxonomy terms, or nodes, these headers could easily exceed 8190 bytes, the hardcoded per-header-line limit in Apache. Apache would silently return a 500 error with no PHP-level exception and no Drupal log entry, producing a blank page that was extremely difficult to diagnose. This fix makes Drupal automatically split the header value across multiple lines with the same header name whenever the value exceeds 8000 bytes, preventing the server error.

Impact

No upgrade or configuration changes required.

Technical details

The fix lives in FinishResponseSubscriber::addDebugCacheHeaders(), a new protected method that replaces the previous inline $response->headers->set() calls for the two debug headers. It sorts the cache tags or contexts, joins them with a single space (the existing separator), and then uses wordwrap() to break the resulting string into lines no longer than the new RESPONSE_HEADER_LINE_MAX_LENGTH = 8000 constant. The resulting array is passed to $response->headers->set($header, $headers), which Symfony's ResponseHeaderBag emits as multiple header lines with the same name. A companion constant RESPONSE_HEADER_CACHE_ITEM_SEPARATOR = ' ' documents the space-separated format (RFC 9110 allows multiple same-name fields, which a receiver is supposed to treat as comma-joined, but these debug headers intentionally use spaces for readability). Apache merges repeated header lines into one with , between them; nginx sends them as separate lines. Either way the server no longer hits the per-line byte limit. The change has no effect when http.response.debug_cacheability_headers is false, which is the default in production.

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: January 18, 2017
  • Committed: May 12, 2026 (9 years and 6 commits later)

Key contributors

  • fgm (OSInet)
  • regilero
  • vaza18 (JAKALA (formerly FFW))
  • charginghawk (Pegasystems)
  • abrar_arshad
  • mxr576 (Pronovix)
  • smustgrave (Mobomo)
  • rgpublic
  • samlerner (CivicActions)
  • tunic (Metadrop)
  • b_sharpe (ImageX)
  • scott_euser (Soapbox)
  • prudloff (Insite)
  • quietone (PreviousNext)
  • erickbj (CI&T)
  • godotislate (Digital Polygon)
  • wim leers (Acquia)
  • mr.baileys (Dropsolid)
  • bappa.sarkar (Weight Watchers)
  • Steven McCoy
  • akz (EWORX S.A.)
  • luke.leber
  • nicxvan (nLightened Development LLC)
  • benstallings (Encore Healthcare)
  • oily

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.

item.title: #3308877: Add static cache for loadEntityByUuid function to store uuid-id pairs in memory
item.description:
• Project: Drupal Core
  • Type: Normal task
  • Version: 11.x-dev
  • Status: Fixed
  • Changes: 6 files, +54/−40 · php +54/−40
  • Diff: f56d20e
  • Discussion: #3308877 · 8 contributors · 31 comments
  • Followers: 18

Summary

Every call to EntityRepository::loadEntityByUuid() previously issued a SQL query to look up the entity ID from the UUID before loading the entity by ID. On pages that look up the same entity by UUID multiple times, or that resolve many entity references (as JSON:API does), this produced a large number of redundant queries. A static in-memory cache now stores UUID-to-ID pairs so that only the first lookup for a given UUID requires a query. The cache is also primed whenever any entity is loaded by ID, so UUID lookups that follow a normal entity load are free. The JSON:API performance tests confirm the improvement: one scenario dropped from 25 queries to 17, another from 14 to 6.

Impact

No upgrade or configuration changes required.

Technical details

EntityStorageBase gains a new $uuidMemoryCacheTag property (entity.memory_cache:uuid:{entityTypeId}) that allows the UUID lookup cache to be invalidated independently from the main entity memory cache.

loadByProperties() now intercepts calls where the only filter is a single UUID string. It checks a uuid_lookup:{entityTypeId}:{uuid} entry in the memory cache before running the EntityQuery. Both positive results (an array of entity IDs) and empty results (no entity found) are stored, so repeated calls with the same UUID are served entirely from memory. setStaticCache() also writes these uuid_lookup entries whenever entities are stored by ID, priming the UUID cache as a side effect of ordinary entity loads.

resetCache() invalidates the entire UUID lookup cache tag ($uuidMemoryCacheTag) whenever any entity ID's cache is cleared, because there is no way to reverse-map a given ID back to its UUID without an additional query. When resetting all caches both the entity and UUID tags are invalidated together.

A required adjustment was made to LruMemoryCache::invalidateTags(): it now removes items from the cache immediately when their tags are invalidated, instead of marking them as invalid and keeping them in memory. This is necessary for the UUID lookup cache to work correctly, since empty-result entries must be evictable via tag invalidation when an entity is saved or deleted. The change also means LruMemoryCache no longer returns tag-invalidated items when $allow_invalid = TRUE is passed to get().

The UninstallDefaultContentTest was updated to obtain fresh storage instances after module install and uninstall rather than calling resetCache() on stale instances, which the more aggressive UUID cache invalidation exposed.

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: September 9, 2022
  • Committed: May 12, 2026 (3 years and 2 commits later)

Collaboration

larowlan opened the MR after profiling a JSON:API endpoint backed by a custom OpenSearch-based entity storage, where each lookup carried additional latency. In that environment, reducing UUID queries dropped a single collection page from 550 queries to 55. catch stepped in to fix test failures: the initial implementation cached empty results, which broke tests that checked for a non-existent UUID before creating an entity. catch's resolution introduced the separate $uuidMemoryCacheTag so the UUID lookup cache could be reset aggressively on any entity save, enabling safe caching of empty results. That fix in turn required modifying LruMemoryCache::invalidateTags() to remove items outright rather than marking them invalid. catch also noted that priming the UUID cache from setStaticCache() was a particularly nice touch, causing every UUID query for entities already loaded by ID to disappear entirely from the JSON:API performance test results.

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.

item.title: #3585555: Bump PHPStan & friends to latest release
item.description:
• Project: Drupal Core
  • Type: Normal task
  • Version: 11.x-dev
  • Status: Fixed
  • Changes: 8 files, +25/−117 · php +18/−110 · json +7/−7
  • Diff: 5200a34
  • Discussion: #3585555 · 7 contributors · 31 comments
  • Followers: 8

Summary

Drupal core bumps mglaman/phpstan-drupal (to 2.0.15) and phpstan/phpstan (to 2.1.54) along with related static-analysis packages, and applies the code fixes those newer versions revealed. The most significant change is a correction in the dependency-injection autowiring layer that eliminates PHP 8.4 deprecation notices for sites whose services use AutowireTrait. The change was committed to both main and 11.x.

Impact

No upgrade or configuration changes required.

Technical details

The functional code change is in AutowireInstanceTrait::getAutowireArguments(). The old call $parameter->allowsNull() directly on a ReflectionParameter instance is deprecated in PHP 8.4; it is replaced with $parameter->getType()?->allowsNull() || $parameter->getType() === NULL. The null-safe operator handles parameters that carry no type hint at all (where getType() returns null), preserving the original behaviour of setting such arguments to null.

Two link module field-formatter classes (LinkFormatter and a sibling formatter) gain @phpstan-param \Drupal\Core\Field\FieldItemListInterface<\Drupal\link\LinkItemInterface> $items docblock annotations on their viewElements() methods. These PHPStan generics annotation let the analyser infer the concrete item type without requiring runtime assert() calls, resolving unknown-variable-type warnings introduced by the stricter PHPStan 2.x series.

The PHPStan baseline file core/.phpstan-baseline.php loses a large number of now-resolved suppressions: missingType.return entries for generated trait*() proxy methods across BlockBase, BlockManager, ContentEntityDeleteForm, FieldTypePluginManager, ModerationStateFieldItemList, workspaces Query/QueryAggregate, and others; and variable.undefined entries across several modules and tests that the upgraded analyser can now prove are always defined. A small number of new baseline entries are added where the stricter analysis flags previously unchecked patterns (for example, an isset.offset check on a known array shape in BlockListBuilder and a variable.undefined for $fiber in RendererTest).

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: April 18, 2026
  • First commit: May 9, 2026 (20 days later)
  • Last commit: May 12, 2026 (2 days and 2 commits later)

Key contributors

  • mondrake
  • xjm (Zoocha)
  • longwave (Full Fat Things)
  • catch (Third and Grove)

Collaboration

During review, xjm flagged that adding assert() calls for link items could disrupt CI pipelines if non-link items had ever been permitted there. mondrake explained that the downstream buildUrl() call already enforces the type natively, making a runtime failure on non-link items inevitable regardless. This led mondrake to switch from assertions to PHPStan generics annotations, which satisfied the type-analysis requirement without adding runtime overhead. longwave separately identified the ReflectionParameter::allowsNull() deprecation warning spreading through anything using AutowireTrait and proposed the one-line fix that was then incorporated.

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.

item.title: #3559132: FileSystem::deleteRecursive() leaves files/directories when realpath() returns FALSE
item.description:
• Project: Drupal Core
  • Type: Critical bug report
  • Version: 10.6.x-dev
  • Status: Fixed
  • Changes: 2 files, +108/−2 · php +108/−2
  • Diff: bd95017
  • Discussion: #3559132 · 9 contributors · 19 comments
  • Followers: 23

Summary

A regression introduced in Drupal 10.5.x caused FileSystem::deleteRecursive() to silently skip all deletions when the target path uses a remote stream wrapper such as S3fs, Azure Blob Storage, or Flysystem. The method returned TRUE claiming success while leaving all files and directories untouched in remote storage. This broke image style flushing (drush image-flush), cache management for derivative files, and any code that uses deleteRecursive() on a remote URI, and posed a risk of sensitive private files remaining in storage after deletion was requested. The fix is now committed to 10.6.x, 11.x, 11.3.x, and main.

Impact

Affects site owners.

  • Security: Files targeted by deleteRecursive() on remote stream wrapper URIs (S3fs, Azure, Flysystem) were silently not deleted and falsely reported as deleted, potentially leaving sensitive private files in storage.

Technical details

The root cause was a change made in #3482449 to prevent symlink traversal attacks. That fix added a realpath() call at the top of deleteRecursive() to resolve stream wrapper URIs to absolute local paths, so that is_link() checks would work correctly. However, FileSystemInterface::realpath() is documented to return FALSE for remote URIs, and the code unconditionally assigned the result back to $path. With $path set to FALSE, the subsequent file_exists(FALSE) check returned FALSE, the method hit its early return, and exited without deleting anything.

The fix tightens the assignment to only apply when realpath() returns a non-false value: $realpath = $this->realpath($path); if ($realpath !== FALSE) { $path = $realpath; }. Local stream wrappers that return a real path still get resolved to local filesystem paths, preserving the symlink traversal protection from #3482449. Remote stream wrappers that return FALSE keep their original URI, which is sufficient because is_link() cannot detect symlinks through those wrappers anyway, and the underlying remote stream wrapper handles directory traversal correctly.

A new kernel test class FileDeleteRecursiveRemoteTest was added using a DummyRemoteStreamWrapper fixture to cover single-file deletion, empty directories, directories with files, and nested subdirectories via a remote-style wrapper that returns FALSE from realpath().

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: November 20, 2025
  • Committed: May 11, 2026 (5 months and 4 commits later)

Key contributors

  • oli4
  • cilefen
  • cmlara (DM13 Security LLC)
  • rory downes
  • jesseh (Ironstar)
  • smustgrave (Mobomo)
  • alexpott (Acro Commerce)

Collaboration

cmlara raised the priority to Critical and made the case for it in a detailed comment, pointing out that the false TRUE return value is particularly dangerous because callers rely on it to confirm deletion of sensitive data (citing real-world usage patterns such as OAuth keys and Google credentials stored under private://). cmlara also noted that PHPStan at level 9 (tracked in #3426047) would have caught this type error, linking the two issues. kim.pepper flagged during review that the fix must not reintroduce the symlink traversal vulnerability from #3482449, and smustgrave verified that the existing tests from that issue still pass with the new patch before pushing it to RTBC.

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.
